author: Pan wenjie
message:
feat: v1.4.3 absorb 3 Session 41 lessons (plan-first / delegation / dimension-check)

Three protocols added to Key Rules + researcher role, all in team-lead
read-once paths (zero cost to spawn prompts).

SKILL.md Key Rules (+2 bullets, EN+CN):
- Large task plan-first: 3+ agents or 5+ tool calls requires
  session-<N>-<topic>-plan.md before any SendMessage; dispatch only
  references plan sections, no re-explaining inline
- Delegate everything delegable: team-lead must NOT self-execute
  commits/push/SSH/cleanup/deploy — these go to backend-dev. team-lead
  keeps decisions, cross-repo coordination, un-delegatable local actions

roles.md researcher (+1 bullet, EN+CN):
- First-round dimension check: team-lead MUST challenge
  "zero side-effect / feasible" conclusions with missed-scenario /
  side-effect-category / usage-dimension questions before accepting

Rejected (too project-specific): cross-repo git remote rule,
nohup/disown startup rule. Already covered: agent message queue
semantics (v1.4.1). User may opt into rejected items via CLAUDE.md
Known Pitfalls per-project.

Net +6 lines across source files; onboarding.md (the per-spawn cost
path) untouched.

author: Pan wenjie
message:
refactor: v1.4.2 — compress v1.4.0/v1.4.1 protocols, cut prose without losing rules

Root cause / Motivation:
  User concern: spawn prompt was growing too large from v1.4.0 additions
  (~45 lines in onboarding.md common template + role sections). Audit
  revealed most of the growth was explanatory prose — rationale, "why",
  elaborations — not action language. LLMs follow tight imperative rules
  better than verbose discursive ones. This pass compresses without
  changing any protocol or removing any rule.

Changes (all files, EN + CN mirror):
  - onboarding.md F2 Reality Check: 10 → 4 lines
  - onboarding.md F3 Multi-Part Recognition: 3 → 1 line
  - onboarding.md F4 Idle ≠ completion report: 10 → 3 lines
  - onboarding.md F6 Contract-First (dev): 17 → 5 lines
  - onboarding.md F1 Anti-Phantom (reviewer): 25 → 8 lines
  - onboarding.md F7 env side effects bullet: tightened
  - roles.md dev Contract-First + env + Doc-Code Sync: 15 → 3 bullets
  - roles.md frontend-dev duplicated block: collapsed
  - roles.md e2e Explore-Then-Codify: 7 → 2 lines
  - roles.md reviewer Anti-Phantom summary: 6 → 1 line (points to full)
  - SKILL.md v1.4.1 Message Delivery Timing: 37 → 18 lines
  - templates.md CLAUDE.md dispatch quick-ref: 12 → 4 lines

Preserved without change:
  - All protocol names, trigger conditions, and actions
  - Memorable anchors (73% phantom rate, file-is-live phrase, etc.)
  - All tag formats ([AUTOMATE], [CLOSED verified], [NOT-FOUND])
  - Templates (api-contracts field table, Known Pitfalls examples)

Numeric results:
  - onboarding.md: 806 → 781 (net v1.4.x growth cut from +45 to +20)
  - roles.md: 350 → 329 (back to v1.3.x baseline)
  - SKILL.md: 569 → 559
  - templates.md: 1047 → 1038

Validation: scripts/validate-release.py → [OK] All checks passed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pan wenjie
message:
docs: v1.4.1 — document Message Delivery Timing Constraint

Root cause / Motivation:
  Empirically verified by user: SendMessage to a spawned teammate is
  NOT delivered mid-turn. The recipient only picks up new messages
  when idle (between turns). You cannot interrupt an agent while it
  is actively working. This is a hard constraint of the spawn/message
  system, not documented in the SendMessage tool description. Team-lead
  dispatch strategy depends on understanding this — without it, leads
  waste time trying to "ping for status", "urgent broadcast stops",
  or "add oh-also-do-X mid-task", all of which silently queue until
  the next idle.

Changes (SKILL.md + templates.md CLAUDE.md section, EN + CN):
  - SKILL.md § Team-Lead Operations Guide: new subsection "Message
    Delivery Timing Constraint" with 6 dispatch consequences and the
    core corollary "files are live, messages are not — read progress.md
    for live state, do not message".
  - templates.md CLAUDE.md Task Dispatch Protocol: short quick-reference
    note pointing to the full guide.

Zero impact on agent spawn prompt size:
  - onboarding.md untouched (spawn prompt source)
  - SKILL.md is read once by team-lead at skill invoke, not in prompts
  - CLAUDE.md template adds ~12 lines to team-lead's main-session
    context; no agent sees this

Expected post-update behavior:
  New teams created via /CCteam-creator will have the timing-constraint
  note in their generated CLAUDE.md. Existing team-leads will benefit
  on their next Step 0 Update Check notification.

Validation: scripts/validate-release.py → [OK] All checks passed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pan wenjie
message:
feat: v1.4.0 — Anti-Phantom, Reality Check, Contract-First, env side effects

Root cause / Motivation:
  Session 39 retrospective surfaced 10 team-execution lessons. After
  deduping project-specific noise, 7 are universal enough to absorb into
  the skill templates. Key pain points: reviewer phantom findings (73%
  rate observed), stale recall after context recovery, multi-part tasks
  silently dropped, field-name drift between parallel devs, missing
  service-restart declarations, empty idle notifications.

Changes (onboarding.md / roles.md / templates.md / SKILL.md, EN + CN):
  1. Reviewer Anti-Phantom Finding Protocol — revive-check open
     findings, require current-commit evidence, forbid "can't find it"
     skips, escalate recurring phantoms via [AUTOMATE]
  2. Reality Check After Recovery — wc/grep/git log before touching
     or reporting on files modified in prior sessions
  3. Multi-Part Task Recognition — acknowledgement must enumerate
     every bundled deliverable; no "done" for partial completion
  4. Idle is not a completion report — every task ends with an
     outbound SendMessage, not a file write
  5. Contract-First for cross-agent interfaces — field tables defined
     in docs/api-contracts.md before either side codes, with unit
     annotations for ambiguity-prone fields
  6. Environmental side effects in completion reports — mandatory
     declaration: none / done-by-me / needs-lead-action
  7. E2E Explore-Then-Codify (soft recommendation in roles.md) —
     interactive MCP exploration before writing specs for unknown UI
  8. SKILL.md phase pre-flight check bullet
  9. templates.md docs/api-contracts.md now ships an example field
     table with unit annotations
  10. templates.md CLAUDE.md Known Pitfalls ships with KP-1/KP-2
      example entries (stateful-service-restart, DEV_MODE bypass)

Expected post-update behavior:
  New teams created via /CCteam-creator will inherit these protocols
  in their onboarding prompts. Existing teams are unaffected until
  rebuild at a phase boundary.

Validation: scripts/validate-release.py → [OK] All checks passed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pan wenjie
message:
docs: v1.3.3 — release guide + validation script + manual-install doc fixes

Infrastructure improvements to prevent regressions like v1.3.0/v1.3.1/v1.3.2.

Added:
- docs/release-guide.md: comprehensive pre-release checklist, common pitfalls
  from actual debugging (frontmatter bugs, name mismatches, CN directory rename,
  version sync), post-release verification, rollback procedure, known upstream
  bugs (#31462, #37590)
- scripts/validate-release.py: automated pre-push validation checking
  1. Version sync across 3 files (plugin.json x2 + marketplace.json)
  2. SKILL.md frontmatter parseability (catches "## name:" Markdown heading
     and missing closing --- that broke v1.3.0 CN)
  3. Three-name alignment (plugin == directory == frontmatter name)
  4. EN/CN parallel file structure
  Tested with version-mismatch injection: correctly fails and exits 1.

Fixed:
- README Option 2 (Manual Install): CN path updated from cn/skills/CCteam-creator
  to cn/skills/CCteam-creator-cn (matches the 1.3.2 directory rename). Added
  explicit guidance on how to invoke (/CCteam-creator) and update (git pull + cp -r).
  Clarified that manual install does NOT require /reload-plugins -- skill appears
  directly in ~/.claude/skills/.
- README Option 3 (Project-level): same CN path fix + added EN option
- README Updating section: same CN path fix

Both README.md and README_CN.md updated in parallel.

Version bump: 1.3.2 -> 1.3.3 (patch, user-visible README path fixes)

Pre-push validation: python scripts/validate-release.py -> [OK] All checks passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pan wenjie
message:
fix: v1.3.2 — align skill name with plugin name for correct namespacing

Root cause (user-discovered):
Claude Code namespaces plugin skills as "plugin-name:skill-directory-name".
Our plugin had mismatched naming:
  - Plugin name:          CCteam-creator
  - Skill directory:      skills/CCteam-creator/
  - Frontmatter name:     setup   ← mismatch (used to be ## name: setup, even worse)

Symptom: Skill(CCteam-creator) → "Unknown skill"
(only Skill(CCteam-creator:CCteam-creator) worked — per-namespace lookup)

Fixes:
1. EN SKILL.md: name: setup → name: CCteam-creator (matches plugin + directory)
2. CN SKILL.md: name: setup → name: CCteam-creator-cn (matches plugin name)
3. Rename cn/skills/CCteam-creator/ → cn/skills/CCteam-creator-cn/
   (CN skill directory now matches plugin name — previously mismatched)
4. Update README slash command examples:
   - /CCteam-creator:setup → /CCteam-creator
   - /CCteam-creator-cn:setup → /CCteam-creator-cn
5. Document Skill(plugin:skill) namespace format for direct tool calls

After this fix, all three identifiers align (like planning-with-files):
  Plugin name == Skill directory name == Frontmatter name field

Expected post-update behavior:
  /CCteam-creator                           → works (slash command)
  Skill(CCteam-creator:CCteam-creator)      → works (explicit namespace)
  "set up a team" (natural language)        → works (description match)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pan wenjie
message:
fix: v1.3.1 — CN SKILL.md frontmatter (unparseable YAML)

The CN skills/CCteam-creator/SKILL.md had a broken frontmatter that
Claude Code's skill loader could not parse:
  - Line 3 was "## name: setup" (Markdown H2 heading) instead of "name: setup"
  - No closing "---" — frontmatter block was never terminated

Result: /plugin install CCteam-creator-cn@ccteam appeared to succeed
but the skill was never registered/discovered by Claude Code.

EN version was already correct (name: setup + closing ---).
This commit aligns CN with EN.

Version bump: 1.3.0 → 1.3.1 (patch, bug fix only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pan wenjie
message:
feat: v1.3.0 — Step 0 update check + bidirectional communication protocol

Major changes:

1. Step 0 Update Check (new, automatic, ~2s)
   - Every skill trigger silently fetches remote plugin.json via WebFetch
   - Compares with local installed version (marketplace cache or ~/.claude/skills/)
   - Prints one-line notification if newer version exists, then immediately continues
   - Zero blocking, zero user confirmation, silent failure on network error

2. Bidirectional communication protocol in common onboarding template
   - "Receive task → confirm understanding" (one-line ack before starting)
   - "Complete report → bring evidence" (grep/diff/test output, not just "done")
   - "Between-task checkpoint" (proactive status before claiming next task)
   - Promoted from dev-only to universal across all roles

3. Universal Escalation Judgment section
   - Moved from dev-only (roles.md) to common onboarding template
   - Added "priority/sequencing unclear" as new must-ask condition
   - Replaced "no options, no ask" anti-pattern with "describe confusion directly"
   - Non-dev roles (researcher/e2e-tester/reviewer/custodian) now have escalation protocol

4. Compact amnesia warning in Step 5
   - Skill proactively warns user before /compact about team-lead memory loss
   - Provides one-sentence rescue command: "Read .plans/<project>/team-snapshot.md"

5. Documentation
   - README Updating section: how to manually update third-party marketplaces
   - README Known Limitation: team-lead amnesia after /compact + rescue command

6. CN golden_rules.py translated to Chinese (cn/ variant consistency)

Version bump: 1.2.0 → 1.3.0 (minor, new protocols + behavioral changes)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pan wenjie
message:
docs: add full lifecycle workflow and enforce snapshot completeness

Snapshot template now explicitly requires COMPLETE, UNABRIDGED onboarding
prompts — no summarization or truncation allowed.

Both READMEs now include a detailed "How It Works — Full Lifecycle"
section with ASCII diagrams covering setup, collaboration, resume,
and phase boundary checks. Placed before the screenshots section
for better discoverability.

author: Pan wenjie
message:
feat: add team snapshot for fast resume without re-reading skill files

On first team creation, saves rendered onboarding prompts to
.plans/<project>/team-snapshot.md with skill source file timestamps.

On resume: if skill files unchanged, spawn agents directly from cached
prompts (~2500 lines skipped). If skill files updated, inform lead
and let them choose fast resume vs full re-read.

Updated Step 0 (resume detection), Step 4 (snapshot generation),
templates (snapshot template + file structure), and both READMEs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pan wenjie
message:
feat: add reviewer calibration protocol and assumption audit mechanism

Reviewer now scores project-specific dimensions (STRONG/ADEQUATE/WEAK) with
anti-leniency rules and calibration anchors. Harness Checklist expanded with
Assumption Audit table for periodic review of load-bearing components.

Inspired by Anthropic's harness design research on evaluator calibration
and the principle that every harness component encodes a model limitation
assumption worth stress-testing.

author: Pan wenjie
message:
feat: e2e-tester reminds team-lead about custodian audit on test pass

Neutral, non-directive reminder at the end of test completion reports.
Team-lead decides whether to activate custodian based on project state.

author: Pan wenjie
message:
feat: replace cleaner with custodian role — constraint engineering upgrade

- Rename cleaner → custodian (管家) with 4 responsibility modules:
  1. Constraint compliance auditing (proactive, not reactive)
  2. Documentation governance (docs/index.md navigation map)
  3. Pattern → Automation pipeline (reviewer [AUTOMATE] → check scripts)
  4. Code cleanup (retained from cleaner)
- Add docs/index.md as dynamic navigation map with section-level line ranges
  (CLAUDE.md is not hot-loaded, so dynamic nav info lives in docs/index.md)
- Add incremental audit awareness — custodian maintains own records,
  does delta checks instead of full scans on every initialization
- Add processing protocol: index/navigation self-fix, content issues report to lead
- Add check script error message standard ([WHAT] + [WHERE] + [HOW TO FIX])
- Expand Step 3.6 from CI skeleton to full Harness Setup
- Add reviewer [AUTOMATE] tag for pattern → automation handoff
- Custodian recommended for 4+ agent teams or long-running projects
- Sync all changes across EN + CN (10 files modified)

author: Pan wenjie
message:
feat: auto-detect existing project and offer resume before setup

Add Step 0: check if .plans/ exists before starting fresh setup.
If found, read CLAUDE.md roster, offer resume vs new project.
No new slash command needed — works with existing triggers.
Closes #1

author: Pan wenjie
message:
feat: default all roles to sonnet, opus only when justified

All roles now default to sonnet. Upgrade to opus only when: user requests it,
cost is not a concern, or role handles critical/complex logic. Updated across
SKILL.md, roles.md, templates.md, and both READMEs.

author: Pan wenjie
message: fix: reframe completion report rule — about lead decision quality, not compression

author: Pan wenjie
message:
feat: require self-contained completion reports from teammates

Teammates must include what they did, where docs are (path + line range),
and key decisions — not just "done". Lead's context gets compressed,
so vague messages lose all value after compaction.

author: Pan wenjie
message:
docs: clarify 200k vs 1M context behavior for teammates

200k (default) auto-compacts fine. 1M cannot auto-compact, costs more,
and performance degrades. Recommend 200k for team projects.

author: Pan wenjie
message:
docs: document teammate context compaction limitation and workaround

Teammates cannot /compact their context. Recommended: exit + claude --continue,
then re-spawn teammates with clean context. All progress safe in .plans/ files.
